provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Trust Property Control Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation

definitions.definition: a Bank licensed in terms of the Banking Act (Cap. 46:04)
assigned to it under the Financial Intelligence Act (Cap. 08:07)
a person entitled to any benefit from the trust and may include the settlor or trustee in the concerned trust
a building society registered in terms of the Building Societies Act (Cap. 42:03)
a trust created or governed by foreign law which seeks to administer and/or dispose trust property located in Botswana
a person who is a non-resident, appointed as a trustee by or in accordance with the trust instrument of a foreign trust where such person administers or disposes trust property located in Botswana
a bank, building society or a non-bank financial institution
assigned to it under the Proceeds and Instruments of Crime Act (Cap. 08:03)
a non-bank financial institution licensed in terms of the Non-Bank Financial Institutions Regulatory Authority Act (Cap. 46:08)

a trust that primarily engages in raising or disbursing funds for purposes such as charitable, religious, cultural, educational, social or fraternal, or for the carrying out of other types of work including, but not limited to—
( a ) the prevention or relief of poverty
a deed registered in terms of the Deeds Registry Act (Cap. 33:02)

any person to whom powers or interests ordinarily exercised by trustees have been vested in him or her, whether it be the settlor or any other person, including the power to—
( a ) direct a trustee to dispose of, advance, lend, invest, pay or apply trust property
the register of trusts, trustees, and trust service providers established and maintained under section 4
a person who makes or arranges for the making of a trust instrument under which the person&rsquo

a trust, non-profit trust, a foundation or any other arrangement through which the ownership in property of one person is by virtue of a trust instrument made over or bequeathed—
( a ) to another person, the trustee, in whole or in part, to be administered or disposed of according to the provisions of the trust instrument for the benefit of the person or class of persons designated in the trust instrument or for the achievement of the object stated in the trust instrument

any person, including the founder or settlor of a trust who acts as trustee by virtue of an authorisation under section 7 and includes any person whose appointment as trustee is already in force at the commencement of the Act
a written agreement, testamentary writing, court order or a notarial deed according to which a trust is created
assigned to "property" under the Proceeds and Instruments of Crime Act
a person, other than a person or business listed under Schedule I of the Financial Intelligence Act, that as part of his or her business, provide any of the following services to a third party—
